text: Ka kɛ ka tʋmtʋm pu'ab la nɛ ba biis la gaad tuon ka Lea nɛ o biis dɔl ka Rakɛl nɛ o biig Josef kpɛlim nya'aŋ.

ref: Jacob himself walked in front of them all, bowing to the ground seven times as he came near his brother.
text: Ka o mɛŋ gaad tuonnɛ igin teŋin nɔɔra ayɔpɔi ka nyaan paae o bier la.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Ka Esau zɔ tɔ'ɔtɔ tʋ'ʋs o na ka kuak o ka nɔk o nu'usi vil o niŋgɔɔnr ka pʋ'ʋs o sʋ'ʋŋa; ka ba wʋsa kum.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Ka Esau gɔs ka nyɛ pu'ab nɛ biis la, ka bu'os o ye, “Anɔ'ɔnamɛ nwana dɔlli fɔ?” Ka Jakob lɛbis ye, “Bibama ka Wina'am nɔki tisim, m bierɛ.”

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Ka tʋmtʋm pu'ab nɛ ba biis la yʋ'ʋn paae na igin teŋin.

ref: Next, Leah and her children came and bowed down; finally, Joseph and Rachel also came and bowed down.
text: Ka Lea nɛ o biis mɛ kena igin teŋin, ka li nya'aŋ ka Josef nɛ Rakɛl mɛ paae na igin teŋin.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Ka Esau bu'os ye, “Ka bʋnkɔnbid banɛ wʋsa ka m tʋ'ʋsi ba suorin la gbin an bɔ?” Ka Jakob lɛbis ye, “M zugdaana, m mɔri ba na ye m tisif ka fʋ di suguru.”

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Ka Esau yɛl ye, “Mam pʋn mɔr linɛ siakim. Kɛl ka fʋn mɔr si'el la nan kpɛn aan fʋ din.”

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Ka Jakob lɛbis ye, “Ayei, m bɛlimnɛ, fʋ sʋnf ya'a sid ma'ae nɛ mam, fʋn di'em m piini nwa'. Mam nyɛɛ fʋ nindaa la li nwɛnɛ m nyɛnɛ Wina'am nɛ, ka li anɛ fʋn di'em sʋ'ʋŋa la zug.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: M bɛlimnɛ, di'em piini linɛ ka m mɔr na la; Wina'am sʋŋim ka m paam linɛ siakim.” Ka Jakob muk hali, ka Esau ti gʋ'ʋŋi siak ka di'e la'ad la.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”
text: Ka Esau yɛl ye, “Ti kem! M na dɔllif.”

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Amaa Jakob lɛbis ye, “Ayei, m zugdaana! Fʋ mi' ye biis la nam pɔɔd, ka li mɛ sʋm ye m gɔsid pɛ'ɛs nɛ na'abanɛ nan mu'asidi ba biis la. Ti ya'a ye ti mugusi ba keŋ baa daar yinne, bʋnkɔnbid la na kpi wʋsa.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: M bierɛ, fʋn gaadim tuon ka mam mɛ kpɛlim nya'aŋi dɔl bʋnkɔnbid la bi'el-bi'el, nɛ biis la, hali nɛ tinamɛ na paae fʋ san'an Seir teŋin na.”

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Ka Esau lɛbisi yɛl o ye, “Ya'a aan ala, fʋn kɛl ka m basim dap sieba ka ba sʋŋif ka gurif.” Ka Jakob ye, “M bierɛ, m pʋ'ʋs ya, amaa li sia'al ya.”

ref: So Esau left for Edom.
text: Ka Esau gban'e o suori lɛb Seir teŋin.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Ka Jakob mɛ gaadi keŋ Sukot teŋin. Anina o da mɛ' yir nɛ gariki kpɛn'ɛs o bʋnkɔnbid la, dinzug ka ba buon zin'ig kan la ye Sukof la.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Jakob n yi Padanaram la nya'aŋ la o da paae Sekɛm tempʋʋgin dinɛ bɛ Keenan sʋ'ʋlimin la nɛ laafi. Ka yi kpikpɛŋ bi'ela n zin'in anina.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Jakob da da' paalʋ Hamor onɛ da an Sekɛm saam la san'an anzurifa kɔbiga. O da ti' sʋga anina zin'in.

ref: then he set up his tents and built an altar there to honor the God of Israel.
text: Anina ka o mɛ' maan bimbim ka buoli li yʋ'ʋr ye El Elohe Israel.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Lea da du'a bipuŋ sɔ' tis Jakob ka o yʋ'ʋr buon Daina. Daar yinne ka Daina da yii keŋ ye o pʋ'ʋs teŋ la pu'a sieba.

ref: She was seen by Hamor's son Shechem, the leader of the Hivites, and he grabbed her and raped her.
text: Hamor da anɛ Hivi buudi ka di teŋ la na'am, ka o biribiŋ Sekɛm nyɛ Daina ka o ya'am kpɛn' o ka o san'am o.

ref: But Shechem was attracted to Dinah, so he told her how much he loved her.
text: Ka o ya'am wʋsa kpɛn' Daina, onɛ an Jakob bipuŋ la, ka o nɔŋ pu'asadir la hali ka pian' nɛ kʋkɔmalisiŋi tis o.

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Ka Sekɛm yɛl o saam Hamor ye, “Li'am pu'a kaŋa tisim ka m dii o.”

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Saŋkanɛ ka Jakob wʋm ye Sekɛm bibig o biig Daina la, o biis da nan bɛ nɛ mɔɔgin kem niigi. Ka o sin baanlimm gur biis la ka ba kul na.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Saŋkan la ka Jakob biis yi mɔɔgini paae na ka wʋm dinɛ maal la, ka li daami ba ka ba sʋʋnr pɛlig bɔzugɔ Sekɛm diis nɛ Israel nyan on san'am Jakob pu'ayua la, li pʋ sʋm ye nɛ' maali nɛ.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Amaa ka Hamor yɛli ba ye, “M biig Sekɛm sʋʋnr kpɛn' fʋ bipuŋ la nɛ hali. M sɔsidif ye fʋ tis o ka o lieb o pu'a.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Kɛl ka ti di taaba biis. Tisimi ti ya pu'asada la ka tinam mɛ tisi ya ti pu'asada.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Ya na tun'e zin' teŋin la nɛ ti, ka nyaŋi tɔlim bɛɛ da' paalʋ ka li na aan ya din.”

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Ka Sekɛm mɛ da bɛɛ ani ka yɛl Daina saam nɛ o tanp ye, “Siakimi ka m na tisi ya linɛ wʋsa ka ya bɔɔd.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Amaa Sekɛm daa san'ami ba taʋn Daina la zug, Jakob biis la da ma' lɛbis on nɛ o saam Hamor

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: ye, “Tinam kʋ nyaŋi tʋm tʋʋm kaŋa; ti kʋ nyaŋi nɔki ti taʋn tis dau kanɛ pʋ kpɛn' baŋʋ; lin aan ti kisʋg la zug.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Yɛl yinne ma'aa na kɛ ka ti siak. Ka li anɛ ye asɛɛ ka ya siaki lieb wʋʋ tinam ka kpɛn'ɛsi ya dap la wʋsa baŋʋ.

ref: Then your families can marry into ours, and ours can marry into yours, and we can live together like one nation.
text: Ya ya'a niŋ ala ti na nɔki ti tanpi tisi ya, ka mɛ nɔki ya tanp la, ka ti na bɛ ya san'an ka tinam nɛ yanam na an buudi yinne.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Amaa ya ya'a pʋ siak ye ya kpɛn' baŋʋ, tinam na nɔki ti taʋn keŋ.”

ref: Hamor and Shechem liked what was said.
text: Ka ban yɛl si'em la an sʋ'ʋŋa tis Hamor nɛ o biig Sekɛm.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Dasaŋ la da pʋ gu'usɛ ka kpɛn' baŋʋ tɔ'ɔtɔ bɔzugɔ, o ya'am da kpɛn' Daina nɛ hali. O mɛ da anɛ onɛ ka o yidim wʋsa tisid o na'asi.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Ka Hamor nɛ o biig Sekɛm keŋ tempʋʋg la za'anɔɔrin ka pian' tis o nidib la ye,

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: “Dap la anɛ ti zuanam. Kɛl ka ba bɛɛ ti teŋin la ka tɔlim, bɔzugɔ teŋ la paalʋ zu'oya ka ba tun'e bɛ. Ti na tun'e dii ba bipumis la ka bam mɛ dii ti bipumis.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Amaa linɛ na kɛ ka dap nwa' siaki bɛ nɛ ti anɛ ye asɛɛ ka ti dap la wʋsa kpɛn' baŋʋ wʋʋ ban nɛ.

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Yanamɛ nyɛ nwa', ba bʋnkɔnbid nɛ ba la'ad wʋsa kʋ lieb tinam bʋnnɛɛ? Kɛl ka ti siak ka ba bɛ kpɛla.”

ref: Every grown man followed this advice and got circumcised. Dinah's Brothers Take Revenge
text: Ka dap banɛ wʋsa yi za'anɔɔrin la ka wʋm Hamor nɛ Sekɛm yɛl si'el la siak ka ba kpɛn'ɛs dap wʋsa baŋʋ.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Ka daba atan' nya'aŋ ka ba wʋsa digi ka li zabidi ba, ka Jakob biribis ayi' Simeon nɛ Levi, banɛ an Daina mabiis la, nɔki ba sʋ'ʋsi keŋi niŋ dap la kʋʋb diin, kʋʋ ba wʋsa.

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: Ba mɛ da kʋ Hamor nɛ Sekɛm wʋsa ka yisi ba taʋn Daina Sekɛm yin la mɔri kul.

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Ka Jakob biis la kena nyɛ ka ba wʋsa kpi la, ka ba vaae teŋ la la'ad la wʋsa, ban san'ami ba taʋn la zug.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ka biis la kadi ba bʋnkɔnbid banɛ an niigi, nɛ bʋmis nɛ pɛ'ɛs la ka faaɛn linɛ wʋsa bɛ tempʋʋgin nɛ pɔɔdin la.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Ba da vaae ba areza'as nɛ pu'ab nɛ biis nɛ bʋn linɛ bɛɛ ba yaan wʋsa.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Ka Jakob yɛl Simeon nɛ Levi ye, “Ya bɔnɛ yɛli tisim. Keenan dim nɛ Perizi dim banɛ bɛ kpɛla la kisig mam. Ti mɛ pʋ zu'oe, ba ya'a la'as taaba ye ba zab nɛ ti, ba na kʋ mam nɛ m yidim la wʋsa.”

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Ka ba lɛbis ye, “Fʋn nyɛ la li an sʋm ye o maali ti taʋn la wʋʋ pu'agɔɔndirɛɛ?”

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: Kɛlli ka ti duoe lɛb Betel ka mɛ' maan bimbimi tis Wina'am onɛ ka m bɛ yɛla ni ka o sʋŋim hali ka bɛ nɛ m zin'ikanɛ wʋsa ka m keŋ la.”

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.
text: Ka ba vaae bada nɛ wina la nɛ tʋblɛma la wʋsa tis Jakob ka o nɔki pin' titita'ar gbinnin Sekɛm tempʋʋgin.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Ka ba yii lɛbid ka Wina'am dabiem kpɛn' tempʋʋg la dim wʋsa, ka ba pʋ kadi baa.

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Anina ka o mɛ' maan bimbim ka buol zin'ig la ye El Betel, bɔzugɔ anina ka Wina'am da nie o mɛŋi pa'al o, on da zɔti yiti o bier san'an la.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Saŋkan la ka Debora onɛ an Rakɛl biyuol la da kpi, ka ba mum o titita'ar kanɛ bɛ Betel la gbinnin, ka ba pʋd zin'ig la ye Allon-Bakuf.

ref: After Jacob came back to the land of Canaan, God appeared to him again. This time he gave Jacob a new name and blessed him by saying: I am God All-Powerful, and from now on your name will be Israel instead of Jacob. You will have many children. Your descendants will become nations, and some of the men in your family will even be kings.
text: Ka Wina'am lɛn yɛl o ye, “Manɛ an Wina'am Onɛ Galis la. Du'ami biis ka nɔbigi bɛdegʋ. Fʋ na wiak buudi bɛdegʋ hali ka ba lieb tembɛda. Na'anam na yii fʋ tuŋʋn na.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Teŋ kan ka m da tis Abraham nɛ Isaak la, m mɛ na nɔki li tis fʋn nɛ fʋ yaas banɛ dɔlli fʋ nya'aŋ la.”

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Ka Jakob yʋ'ʋn nɔk kugiri ba'a zi'el sappi, zin'ikanɛ ka Wina'am pian' tis o la, ka bu'oe daam nɛ malʋŋ kpaam niŋi li zug,

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: ka buol zin'ikanɛ ka Wina'am pian' tis o la ye Betel. Rakɛl kum yɛla

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Ka ba keŋ bi'ela yi Betel ka nam pʋ paae Efirata teŋin naa ka Rakɛl sa'ae ka li wʋm o hali.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Ka saŋkanɛ ka li wʋm o hali la, ka pu'adu'as la yɛl o ye, “Da zɔt dabiem ka ɔŋa mɛ anɛ biribiŋ.”

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Ka on bɔɔd kum la ka pʋd o biribiŋ la yʋ'ʋr ye Ben-Oni. Amaa ka o saam da pʋd o ye Benjamin.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Ka Rakɛl kpi ka ba mum o suor linɛ ken Efirata teŋin kpikpɛŋ, ani ka ba buon Betilehem nannanna la.

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Ka Jakob nɔk kugiri ba'a zi'el sappi o yaʋg zug, ka li nan kpɛm bɛ zina pa'an Rakɛl yaʋg la zin'ig.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Ka Israel lɛn keŋ bi'ela paae Migdaleda teŋin zin'in. Jakob biis yɛla (

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Israel n da bɛ teŋ kan la, ka Reuben digin nɛ o saam pu'abil Biliha ka Israel wʋmi li yɛla. Jakob da mɔr biribis pii nɛ ayi':

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Ka Jakob kuli o saam Isaak san'an Mamir linɛ kpi'e nɛ Kiriaf Ariba teŋin linɛ an Hebron, zin'ikanɛ ka Abraham nɛ Isaak da zin' la.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Esau da dii o pu'ab la ka ba an Keenan dim. O da di Ada onɛ saamme an Elon onɛ an Hitti nid la, nɛ Oholibama ka on mɛ an Ana pu'ayua ka an Zibeon yaaŋ onɛ an Hivi nid.

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: Ka o da di Basemaf ya'as, onɛ an Isimael pu'ayua ka an Nebaiof taʋn.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: ka Oholibama du'a Jeus nɛ Jalam nɛ Kora. Bama da an Esau biis banɛ ka o da du'a Keenan sʋ'ʋlimin la.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Esau da nɔk o pu'ab nɛ o biis nɛ o yidim wʋsa nɛ o niigi nɛ pɛ'ɛs nɛ bʋnkɔnbid wʋsa, la'an nɛ la'abanɛ ka o paam Keenan teŋin la wʋsa, duoe keŋ teŋ kanɛ lal nɛ o pitʋ Jakob.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Ba la'ad la da zu'oe nɛ galis ka ba kʋ nyaŋi la'am bɛɛ. Ban da bɛ teŋ si'a la da pɔɔd ka pʋ siaki babayi' la, ba bʋnkɔnbid la zu'oe la zug.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Ala ka Esau , da keŋ zin' Seir zuoya teŋin la.

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Esau yaas banɛ da an na'anam la nwa': Elifaz da an Esau bikpɛŋ ka o biis banɛ da di na'am la anɛ: Teman nɛ Omar nɛ Zefo nɛ Kenas

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: nɛ Kora nɛ Gatam nɛ Amalek. Bama da aan o yaas banɛ an na'anam Edom sʋ'ʋlimin la ka yi Elifaz tuŋʋn na ka an Ada yaas.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Esau pu'a Oholibama biis banɛ da di na'am nwa': Jeus nɛ Jalam nɛ Kora. Bama da an na'anamɛ yi Esau pu'a Oholibama onɛ an Ana pu'ayua la tuŋʋn na.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Bama da an Esau biribis, banɛ da aan ba na'anam. Seir yaas yɛla (

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Seir, onɛ an Hor nid la, da dɛŋi bɛ Seir teŋin la, ka o biribis an Lotani nɛ Soobal nɛ Zibeon nɛ Ana

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotani biribis nwa': Hori nɛ Hemam. Timna da anɛ Lotani taʋn.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Soobal biribis da anɛ: Alivan nɛ Manahaf nɛ Ebal nɛ Siefo nɛ Onam.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Zibeon biribis da anɛ: Aya nɛ Ana. Ana kaŋa da bɛ sian'arin kemmi o saam Zibeon bʋmis ka nyɛ ka ku'otʋʋlʋg tans teŋin yit na la.

ref: Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.
text: Ana biisi nwa': Diseon nɛ Oholibama onɛ da na Ana pu'ayua la.

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Diseon biis da anɛ: Hemdan nɛ Esiban nɛ Itiran nɛ Keran.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ezer biribis da anɛ: Bilihan nɛ Zaavan nɛ Akan.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Disean biis da anɛ: Uz nɛ Aran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Bama da an Hor dim na'anam: Lotani nɛ Soobal nɛ Zibeon nɛ Ana.

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Diseon nɛ Ezer nɛ Disean. Bama da an Hor dim na'anam wʋʋ ba dɔɔdi an si'em, Seir teŋin la.

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Baal-Hanan onɛ an Akibo biig la da kpi la, Hada da di'e o zin'igi di na'am la, ka o tempʋʋg la yʋ'ʋr buon Pau, ka o pu'a yʋ'ʋr buon Mehetabɛl ka o ma yʋ'ʋr buon Matired onɛ mɛ an Mezahab pu'ayua.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau took their names from their families and the places where they lived. They are Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text: Na'anam banɛ da yi Esau tuŋʋn na la nwa', li pa'annɛ ba yʋda nɛ ba dɔɔd nɛ ban yi sʋ'ʋlim si'a na. Ba da anɛ: Timna nɛ Aliva nɛ Jetef

ref: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text: nɛ Oholibama nɛ Ela nɛ Pinon

ref: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text: nɛ Kena nɛ Teman nɛ Mibzar

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: nɛ Magdiel nɛ Iram. Bama da an Edom na'anamɛ dɔlis wʋʋ ba wʋsa da zin' teŋ-teŋ si'em la, sɔ' wʋsa nɛ o sʋ'ʋlim. Esau, onɛ an Edom, buudi wʋsa yaab la, yɛla la.

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Jakob da zin'inɛ o saamme da zin'in teŋ si'a, Keenan teŋin la.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Ka dau la lɛbis ye, “Ba tɔɔm ya. M pa'a wʋm ka ba yɛt ye, ‘Kɛlli ka ti keŋ Dotan teŋin.’” Josef da duoe kad o bieya la bugir ka nyɛɛ ba ka ba kpi'e Dotan.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Israel da nɔŋ Josef n gaadi o biis la wʋsa, bɔzugɔ o da du'a o nɛ saŋkanɛ ka o da kʋdig. Ka o da sɛn fukanɛ bɛɛ li kɔn' ka vɛnl hali tis o.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: O bieya la da nyɛ ka ba saam nɔŋ o gaadi ba wʋsa la, ba da kisig o nɛ, ka pʋ bɔɔd o yɛla.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Ka Josef ti zaansim ka tu'as zaansʋŋ la tis o bieya la ka ba da maligim kisig o hali.

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: O da yɛli ba ye, “Kɛlisimi zaansʋŋ kanɛ ka m zaansim.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Man zaansim nɛ, ka ti bɔbid mui bɔn'ɔgin, ka mam mui bɔbir la duoe zi'e sappi, ka yanam mui bɔba la la'asi giligi li ka igini li tuon.”

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: O bieya la da yɛl o ye, “Fʋ tɛn'ɛs ye fʋ na sʋ'oe tinamaa?” Ka maligim kisig o hali, o zaansʋŋ la nɛ on yɛl si'el la zug.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Ka o lɛn zaansim ya'as ka yɛl o bieya la ye, “Kɛlisimi, mam lɛn zaansim nɛ ya'as, ka winnig nɛ nwadig nɛ nwadbibis pii nɛ yinne la'asi igini man tuon.”

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: Ka mɛ yɛl o saam wʋʋ on yɛl o bieya si'em la, ka o saam tans o ka yɛl ye, “Zaansʋŋ bɔ buudi la ka fʋ zaansim? Mam nɛ fʋ ma nɛ fʋ bieya la sid na kena igin fʋn tuonnɛ?”

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: O bieya la da mɔr o nɛ fufum, amaa ka o saam la mɔr nɛ' wʋsa o pʋtɛn'ɛrin. Josef bieya kuos o si'em

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: O bieya la da keŋ zin'ikanɛ kpi'e Sekɛm tempʋʋgin la ye ba kemi ba saam pɛ'ɛs anina.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Ka Israel yɛl Josef ye, “Gɔsima! Fʋ bieya la keŋ ye ba kem pɛ'ɛs la Sekɛm baba bɛɛ bɔ? Kem na ka m tʋmi fʋ ba san'an.” Ka o lɛbis ye, “Tɔ', m wʋm ya.”

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ka o yɛl o ye, “Kem gɔs ye ban nɛ pɛ'ɛs la wʋsa mɔr laafi, ka lɛb na ka m wʋmi ba labaar.” Ka o tʋm o ka o yi Hebron bɔn'ɔgin la keŋ hali ti paae Sekɛm teŋin.

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: Ka dau sɔ' da nyɛɛ o ka o giilimmi giligid karinyana la ni, ka bu'os o ye, “Fʋ ieed bɔ?”

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Ka o lɛbis ye, “M ieednɛ m bieya, fʋ na pa'alim ban kemmi ba kɔnbid la zin'isi'aa?”

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Amaa ban da nyɛɛ o ka o nam pʋ paae ba san'an na la, ba sɔns nɛ taaba n gban'e ye ba kʋʋ o.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Ba da yɛl taaba ye, “Zaansʋŋ sɔb la nwana kenna!

ref: pieces of silver they sold him to the Ishmaelites who took him to Egypt.
text: Kemi na ka ti kʋʋ o lɔs bulbama yinne ni, ka yɛl ye, bʋnkɔnbbɛ'ogʋ gban'e o ɔnb. Ala ka ti na gɔsi o zaansʋŋ la na niŋ si'em!”

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Reuben n da wʋm la, o da mɔ ye o faaɛn o ba nu'usin, ka yɛl ye, “Ti da kʋʋ o.”

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Ka lɛn yɛli ba ye, “Ti da yis ziim. Lɔmminɛ o sian'arin bʋmbɔkin nwa', ka da si'isi nɛ o nɛ ya nu'ugɔ.” Reuben da yɛl nɛ'ɛŋa nɛ ye o faaɛn o yi ba nu'usin mɔri lɛbi tis o saam.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Josef n da paae o bieya san'an na la, ba da yɛɛg o fuvɛnliŋ kanɛ ka o yɛ la

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Ba yʋ'ʋn da zin' ye ba di diib, ka nyɛ Isimael nidib ka ba yi Gilead n gat ka mɔr yʋgʋma ka ziid zɛɛnd banɛ nyuusid nɛ nimmi'isʋg kpaam nɛ tudaari mɔri gati ken Egipt.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Juda da yɛl bama la ye, “Ti ya'a kʋʋ ti pitʋ nwa' su'a di nyɔɔd an bɔ tisi ti?

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Reuben n da lɛb bʋmbɔkin la ka Josef kae la, o da aans o fuud sʋnsa'aŋ zug.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: O da lɛb o ba'abiis la san'an yɛl ye, “Biig la kae, nannanna m lɛbid yaanɛ?”

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Ka ba nɔk Josef fuug la, ka kʋ bʋʋgi nɔk fuug la mi'is ziimin la.

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: Ba da mɔr fuvɛnliŋ la lɛbi ba saam san'an yɛl o ye, “Ti pi'ig nɛ'ɛŋa nɛ, di'em gɔs ye li anɛ fʋ biig la fuugɔɔ?”

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ka o baŋi li ka ye, “Li anɛ m biig la fuug! Bʋnkɔnbbɛ'ogʋ gban'e o, Josef sid aansnɛ zɔra-zɔra.”

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Jakob da aaɛn o fuud sʋnsa'aŋ zug, ka yɛ bɔto fuug, ka kaas o biig la yɛla dabisa bɛdegʋ.

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: O biribis nɛ bipumis wʋsa da la'asi bɛlim o, amaa ka o zan'as ka ye, “Ayei, m na kaasi ala ti paae m yaʋgʋn, m biig la zug!” Ala ka o saam la da kum o yɛla hali.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Midian dim la da kuos Josef Egipt teŋin tis Potifa onɛ an Faaro na'ayikpɛm la yinne. O da anɛ banɛ gur na'ab la tuongat.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: Saŋkan la Juda da bas o ba'abiis ka keŋi bɛ dau sɔ' san'an Adullam teŋin, ka o yʋ'ʋr buon Hira.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Ani ka Juda nyɛ Keenan dau onɛ yʋ'ʋri buon Sua la pu'ayua. O da dii o ka digin nɛ o,

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: ka o gban'e pʋʋgʋ du'a biribiŋ ka ba pʋd o, o yʋ'ʋr ye Er.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Ka o lɛn du'a biribiŋi pʋd o, o yʋ'ʋr ye Sela. O da du'a o nɛ Kezib teŋin.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Juda da bɔ pu'a tis Er onɛ aan o bikpɛɛm la. O yʋ'ʋri da buon Tamar.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Amaa Er, onɛ an Juda bikpɛɛm la, da anɛ tʋʋmbɛ'ɛd sɔb Zugsɔb la tuon, alazug Zugsɔb la da kʋʋ o.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Juda da yɛl Onan ye, “Dimi fʋ bier pu'a la, ka maal pʋkɔndiir malima nwɛnɛ lin nar si'em ka li kɛ ka fʋ du'a biisi tisi fʋ bier ka o gbin da li'.”

ref: Onan knew the child would not be his, and when he had sex with Tamar, he made sure that she would not get pregnant.
text: Amaa Onan n da baŋ ye o kʋ sʋ'oe biis la, o ɛɛnti digin nɛ pu'a la ka kpa'ae o daulim bas teŋ, linɛ na kɛ ka o kʋ du'a biisi tis o bier la.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: On da tʋm si'el la da anɛ bugus tʋʋma Zugsɔb la tuon. Ka o mɛ kʋʋ o.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ka Juda da yɛl o bipu'a Tamar ye, “Lɛmmi fʋ saam yiri bɛ nɛ pʋkɔntim la ka m biig Sela ti nɔbig.” O da tɛn'ɛs ye, “Daasi'erɛ Sela na ti kpi wʋʋ o bieya la kpi si'em la.” Tamar da keŋ bɛɛ o saam yin.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Li da yuug ya hali ka Juda pu'a onɛ an Sua pu'ayua la nyaan kpi. Juda sʋnsa'aŋi da gaad la, o da keŋ Timna teŋin, ka o nidib la kiesid o pɛ'ɛs kɔnbid anina. O zua Hira onɛ an Adullam nid la da dɔl o keŋ.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Ba da yɛl Tamar ye, “Fʋ dayaam dau la bɛ suori wa'ae Timna ye o kies o pɛ'ɛs kɔnbid la.”

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Ka o da yɛɛs o pʋkɔnfuud ka tiak fuudi yɛ ka viligi o zug nɛ fubili tiak o mɛŋ ka keŋ zin' Enaim za'anɔɔrin dinɛ bɛ Timna suorin la. Bɔzugɔ, o da nyɛ ka Sela bi', ka ba pʋ kɛ ka o kul o.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Juda nyɛɛ o la, ka tɛn'ɛs ye o anɛ pu'agɔɔndir, on lus o nindaa la zug,

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Ka o yɛl ye, “M na tisif bʋpɔl m kɔnbid la pʋʋgin.” Ka o bu'os o ye, “Bɔ ka fʋ na tisim ka li an kasɛt hali nɛ fʋn na ti mɔr bʋʋg la na tisi ma?”

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Ka o bu'os o ye, “Ka bɔ ka fʋ bɔɔd ye m tisif ka li an kasɛta?” Ka o lɛbis ye, “Nɔkimi fʋ niŋgɔɔnr baŋ kanɛ ka fʋ yʋ'ʋr sɔbi niŋ anina la nɛ fʋ dansaar la tisim.” Ka o zaŋi tis o ka digin nɛ o. Ka o kpɛn gban'e pʋʋg.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Tamar n gaad la, o yɛɛg fukanɛ lus o zug la ka lɛbis o pʋkɔnfuud la yɛ ya'as.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Juda da tʋm o zua onɛ an Adullam nid la nɛ bʋpɔl ye o keŋ tis pu'a la ka di'e o la'abanɛ ka o yɛl nɔɔri zi'el la. Amaa ka o keŋ ka pʋ nyɛɛ o.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ka o bu'os dap banɛ bɛɛ anina la ye, “Teŋgban pu'agɔɔndir kanɛ zin' suor kpikpɛŋ Enaim teŋ za'anɔɔrin la bɛ ya?” Ka ba yɛl o ye, “Teŋgban pu'agɔɔndir ka' kpɛlaa.”

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Ka o lɛb Juda san'an yɛl o ye, “Mam pʋ nyɛɛ o, dinɛ lɛm paas la dap banɛ bɛɛ anina la ye, ‘Teŋgban pu'agɔɔndir ka' kpɛlaa.’”

ref: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
text: Juda da yɛl ye, “On mɔr si'el la kɛl ka o mɔri keŋ; ya'a ka' ala, ba na la'adi ti. Li ya'a an sida, m pʋ pan'as o, bɔzugɔ m tʋm ya ye ba mɔr bʋpɔl la keŋi tis o ka pʋ nyɛɛ o.”

ref: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
text: Nwadis atan' nya'aŋ ka ba yɛl Juda ye, “Fʋ bipu'a Tamar kpɛn' nɛ dap bɔɔb ka ba'asi nɔk pʋʋg.” Juda da yɛl ye, “Mɔri nɛ o yina ka nyu'oe o nɛ bugum ka o kpi.”

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ban mɔr Tamar n yina la, o tʋm labaari yɛl o dayaam dau la ye, “M mɔr nɛ pʋʋg ka li anɛ dau kanɛ sʋ'oe la'abama nwa' tisim.” Ka yɛl ye, “Gɔsim ye fʋ na baŋ la'abama an sɔ' bʋnnɛɛ? Li anɛ baŋ nɛ li nwiig, nɛ dansaar.”

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Juda da baŋya ka ye, “O anɛ pʋpielim sɔbi gaad man. Mam pʋ zaŋ o tisim biig Sela ka o di la zugʋ kɛ ka pu'a la niŋim anwana la.” Juda da pʋ lɛn digin nɛ o ya'asɛ.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: On da du'ad la, ka yinne yis o nu'ug na, ka pu'adu'as la nɔk gum zɛn'ugʋ lɔɔ o nu'ug la ka ye, “Oŋa yi yiiga na.”

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Amaa, on lɛbis o nu'ug la, ka yinne la yina, ka o ye, “Fʋn kɔɔ yi si'em nwanaa?” Ka pʋd o, o yʋ'ʋr ye Perez.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Ka liibir onɛ mɔr gum zɛn'ug o nu'ugin la yʋ'ʋn yina ka ba tis o yʋ'ʋr ye Zera.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Tɔ', ka ba da mɔr Josef n keŋ Egipt ka Potifa onɛ an Faaro na'ayikpɛɛm ka an banɛ gur Na'ab la tuongat la da' o Isimael dim banɛ mɔr o na la san'an.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: o zugdaan la da nyɛ ka Zugsɔb bɛ nɛ o, ka linɛ ka o tʋm wʋsa da kennɛ tuon sʋ'ʋŋa.

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: O sʋnf da ma'ae nɛ o hali ka o kɛ ka o gur o. Potifa da nɔk o yir wʋsa nɛ on mɔr si'el wʋsa yɛla niŋ o nu'usin ka o gɔti li.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: O da zaŋ on mɔr si'el wʋsa gu'ul Josef. Josef n da gɔsid si'el wʋsa la zug, o ba'a da lɛn kae o yir la yɛla nii, ya'a ka' on na di disi'a ma'aanɛ. Josef yʋ'ʋn da nɔb nɛ hali ka vɛnl.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Li da pʋ yuugɛ ka o zugdaan la pu'a ya'am kpɛn' o ka o yɛl o ye, “Kem na digin nɛ m!”

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Amaa o da zan'as ka yɛl o ye, “Gɔsima li ka' manɛ gɔsid si'el wʋsa yiraa? M zugdaan pʋ lɛn gɔsid si'el-si'el yiraa. Bɔzugɔ, o nɔki li wʋsa niŋi m nu'usin.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Sɔ' ka' yin kpɛla gat mam. M zugdaan la pʋ mɔŋim si'el si'ela, asɛɛ fʋn ma'aa, fʋn aan o pu'a la zug. M na niŋ wala maal bugisim kaŋa buudi, tʋm tʋʋmbɛ'ɛdi pɔ'ɔgi m Wina'am?”

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: La'an nɛ on da zan'as la, pu'a la da sin'igid Josef nɛ daar wʋsa, ka o mɛ da zan'as ye o kʋ digin nɛ o bɛɛ li'el o san'anɛ.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Daar yinne ka o kpɛn' yin la ye o tʋm o tʋʋma, ka yammis la sɔ'ɔsɔ ka' yin la.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Ka pu'a la gban'e o fuug ka yɛl ye, “Kem na digin nɛ m!” Ka Josef yɛɛg fuug la bas o nu'usin ka zɔɔ yi.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: pu'a la buol yin la yammis la wʋsa yɛli ba ye, “Gɔsimi ban mɔr Hibiru bikaŋa kena ye o ziemi ti si'em! O kpɛn' kpɛla na ye o digin nɛ mam; ka m kaas kuŋ,

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
text: ka on wʋm ka m kaas ye ba faanmim la, ka o zɔ ka bas o fuugʋ m nu'usin.”

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: O da nɔk fuug la digil ka o sid la ti kul na.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Ka o yɛl o ye, “Hibiru yammʋg kanɛ ka fʋ mɔr na la kena ye o bibigim.

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: Amaa on wʋm ka m kaas ye ba faanmim mɛ bas la, ka o zɔ ka bas o fuugʋ m nu'usin.”

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: ka o mɔr Josef n keŋ yɔ' zin'ikanɛ ka ba yɔɔd na'ab nimbanɛ da tʋm bɛ'ɛd la. Josef n da bɛ sanrega ni la,

ref: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
text: Zugsɔb la da bɛ nɛ o; ka onɛ gur sanrega la ya'am kpɛn' o.

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Onɛ gur sanrega la da kɛ ka Josef sʋ'oe banɛ bɛ sanrega ni la, ka gɔsid linɛ wʋsa tʋm anina.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Onɛ gur sanrega la da pʋ lɛn gɔsid linɛ wʋsa ka o niŋ Josef nu'usin la, bɔzugɔ Zugsɔb la da bɛ nɛ o ka kɛ ka on maal si'el wʋsa ken tuon sʋ'ʋŋa.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: O da nɔki ba yɔ' onɛ an banɛ gur na'ab la tuongat yin, dɔkanɛ ka ba da yɔ' Josef ani la mɛn.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Banɛ gur na'ab la tuongat da nɔki ba niŋ Josef nu'usin ka o gɔsidi ba. Ba da bɛɛ anina ka li yuug bi'ela.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Dapa ayi' nwa', onɛ kuobid na'ab daam nɛ onɛ sʋ'oe bodobodo yɛla la da zaansim yʋ'ʋŋ yinne, ka sɔ' wʋsa zaansʋŋ mɔri li gbine li kɔn'.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Bɛogʋ da nie ka Josef kɛɛn ba san'an na la, o da nyɛ ka ba nindaas zuŋ ka ba sʋnya san'am.

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Ka ba lɛbis ye, “Ti babayi' nwa' sa zaansimnɛ yʋ'ʋŋ, amaa sɔ' kae na pa'ali ti li gbinnɛ.” Ka Josef lɛbis ye, “Abaŋ zaansʋŋ gbin pʋ yit nɛ Wina'am ni naa? Yɛliminimɛ ya zaansʋŋ la!”

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: ka li mɔr wila atan' ka vanpɔla yina ka puum mɛ pu, ka li pʋ yuugɛ ka li wali bi'ig.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: Ka nwam kanɛ ka m mɔri tisid Faaro daam la bɛɛ m nu'ugin ka m nɔk wala la nwiaki niŋ Faaro nwammin la, nɔki niŋ o nu'ugin.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Ka Josef yɛl o ye, “Li gbinni nwa'. Wila atan' la anɛ daba atan'.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Daba atan' nwa' sʋʋgin, Faaro na yisif kpɛla ka kɛ ka fʋ lɛbi tʋmmi fʋ tʋʋma wʋʋ fʋn daa pʋn tʋm si'em la, ka fʋ na nɔk Faaro nwammi niŋ o nu'usin wʋʋ fʋn daa kuobid o daam si'em la.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Amaa si'el wʋsa ya'a keŋ sʋ'ʋŋa tisif, fʋn tɛnri m yɛla ka pa'al pʋpielim nɛ m, pian'ami m yɛla tis Faaro ka kɛ ka m yi sanrega nwa' ni.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ba mukim nɛ ziimɛ yi Hibiru teŋin na, hali kpɛla nwa' m pʋ tʋm si'el dinɛ nar ye m bɛ bʋmbɔk kaŋa nii.”

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Onɛ an bodobodo maannib kpɛɛm la nyɛ ka Josef tu'as o tiraan la yɛla ka li malis la, o yɛl Josef ye, “Mam mɛ sa zaansimnɛ ka bodobodo pɛɛd atan' bɛɛ m zugin,

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: ka pɛ'ɛkanɛ bɛ zugin la, ka bodobodo buudi wʋsa linɛ an Faaro din bɛɛ di ni, amaa ka niis ɔnbid bodobodo la.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Ka Josef yɛl ye, “Li gbinni nwa'. Pɛɛd atan' la anɛ daba atan'.

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: Dabisa atan' nwa' sʋʋgin, Faaro na yisif kpɛla ka fi'igi fʋ zug, ka yuluf tiig zug, ka niis dii fʋ niŋgbina la.”

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Daba atan' daar la da anɛ Faaro du'am tiɛnr dabisir, ka o maal di'ema nɛ ba ka dʋg diibi tisid o na'ayikpɛm. Ka o yis onɛ gɔsid o daam yɛla nɛ onɛ maan o bodobodo la sanrega ni na zi'eli ba o na'ayikpɛm la tuon.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ka yul onɛ aan o bodobodo maannib kpɛɛm la nwɛnɛ Josef n da pa'ali ba zaansʋŋ la gbin si'em la.

ref: but the king's personal servant completely forgot about Joseph.
text: Amaa onɛ sʋ'oe daam yɛla la da tam Josef yɛla nɛ.
